title: Week 7 Lecture PDF: Adding and Manipulating the Data
summary: Lecture PDF for Week 7 with data manipulation examples, SQL command patterns, and database update workflows.

Page 11
SQL DDL (Data Definition Language)
Relatively simple
CREATE TABLE PropertyInspection
(
propNo INT unsigned not null,
inspDate DATE not null,
inspTime TIME not null,
comments VARCHAR not null,
staffNo INT,
PRIMARY KEY(propNo, inspDate),
FOREIGN KEY(staffNo) REFERENCES
Staff(staffNo)
);
VARCHAR: max 65535 bytes
TEXT: no limit
CREATE TABLE Staff
(
staffNo INT,
sName VARCHAR not null,
PRIMARY KEY(staffNo)
);

Page 12
Overview of Data Types
Type
Variations
Meaning
Pay attention to...
Integer
int, tinyint, smallint, bigint
Whole number
Not every number needs to be
of type 'Integer'
Decimal
number, decimal, numeric, float
Number with decimals
Make sure to define precision
Use integer instead of decimal
where you can
Date
date, time, datetime, timestamp
A calendar date. Depends on
locale
Think whether you want time
as well, or just the day.
String
char(n), varchar(n), text, memo
Character strings
Numbers should be strings if
you don’t use them for
calculations (postcodes, phone
numbers)
LOBs
CLOB, BLOB, image, text, memo
For large objects – character
(CLOB) or bits (BLOB)
for pics or large text
Binary
boolean, binary, varbinary
Binary fields and strings of
true/false

Page 14
Null
› is not zero (0)
› is not an empty string ('')
› is not space characters (' ')
› is an unknown value
‘ternary logic’
id number [PK] not null
firstname VARCHAR not null
lastname VARCHAR not null
address VARCHAR not null
gender CHAR
Customer
What is your gender?
Male
Female
Non-binary
Prefer not to say

Page 15
Null
CREATE TABLE Staff (
staffID INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
name VARCHAR (100) NOT NULL,
age TINYINT UNSIGNED,
gender CHAR (1),
PRIMARY KEY (staffID)
);
Find all staff with age > 65?

Page 16
Default
CREATE TABLE Staff (
staffID INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
name VARCHAR (100) NOT NULL,
age TINYINT UNSIGNED,
gender CHAR (1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'U',
PRIMARY KEY (staffID)
);
even better:
gender ENUM ('M', 'F', 'N', 'U') DEFAULT 'U'
